common.siteDescription: Find the best fishing spots in Poland

hero.title: Find Your Perfect Catch
hero.subtitle: FISH.AIR uses advanced weather and environmental data analysis to help anglers in Poland find the best spots and conditions for a successful catch.

features.title: Smart Fishing
features.description: We use weather data, atmospheric pressure, water temperature, and many other factors to predict fish activity in real time.

map.title: Interactive Map
map.description: Browse all fishing waters in Poland on one interactive map. Filter by fish species, conditions, and distance.

alerts.title: Smart Alerts
alerts.description: Receive notifications when conditions are optimal for your favorite fish species in selected locations.

rybopedia.title: Fish Encyclopedia
rybopedia.description: Comprehensive knowledge base about fish found in Polish waters — from biology to fishing techniques.

forecast.title: Bite Forecast
forecast.description: Daily fish activity forecast based on meteorological data and historical bite patterns.

items.name: Marek K.
items.text: FISH.AIR completely changed my approach to fishing. Thanks to the alerts, I never miss optimal conditions. Last season I caught my personal record!

items.name: Anna W.
items.text: The map is incredibly accurate. I found spots I had no idea about. The Rybopedia is a treasure trove of knowledge — I recommend it to anyone starting their fishing journey.

items.name: Tomasz R.
items.text: As a guide, I use FISH.AIR daily. The bite forecasts are surprisingly accurate, and my clients are thrilled with the results. This is the number one tool in my arsenal.

team.title: Our Team
team.description: We are a group of technology and fishing enthusiasts who joined forces to create the tool we always needed ourselves.

cta.title: Ready for Your Next Catch?
cta.description: Join the community of anglers already using FISH.AIR to catch more and smarter.

rybopedia.title: Fish Encyclopedia
rybopedia.description: Comprehensive knowledge base about fish found in Polish waters.

pike.name: Pike
pike.overview: Pike is one of the best-known and most prized freshwater predators. It has an elongated, torpedo-like body and a distinctive flattened snout resembling a duck's bill.
pike.habitat: Prefers vegetated, shallow still and slow-flowing waters. Found in lakes, ponds, oxbow lakes and calm stretches of rivers.
pike.behavior: An ambush predator that attacks violently from cover, using aquatic vegetation as camouflage. Most active at dawn and dusk.
pike.fishing: Effective methods include spinning (jerkbaits, soft plastics, spoons), live-bait fishing and fly fishing. Best results near vegetation and underwater structures.
pike.season: Year-round, except the closed season (1 Jan – 30 Apr). Best bites in autumn and spring.
pike.diet: Fish (roach, perch, bream), frogs, small aquatic mammals and birds. Juveniles feed on invertebrates.
pike.appearance: Elongated body in shades of green and olive with light spots. Dorsal and anal fins shifted towards the tail.
pike.spawning: March–April at 8–12°C, on flooded meadows and shallow floodplains.
pike.bestBait: Jerkbait, soft plastic, spoon, live bait

perch.name: Perch
perch.overview: Perch is a common predator with characteristic striped colouring. One of the most frequently caught species in Polish waters, popular with beginners and experienced anglers alike.
perch.habitat: Inhabits diverse waters — lakes, rivers, ponds, reservoirs. Prefers hard-bottomed areas with underwater structures.
perch.behavior: Juveniles school; adults hunt alone or in small groups. Active mainly during the day.
perch.fishing: Spinning (small jerkbaits, soft plastics, spinners), bottom and float fishing with worm. Takes natural and artificial baits readily.
perch.season: Year-round (no closed season). Best bites from spring to autumn. Active under ice in winter.
perch.diet: Aquatic invertebrates, fry, small fish, eggs of other species.
perch.appearance: Slightly laterally compressed body with characteristic dark vertical bars. Reddish pelvic and anal fins.
perch.spawning: March–May at 7–12°C on shallow, vegetated stretches.
perch.bestBait: Worm, small soft plastic, spinner, spoon
perch.bestTime: All day, especially morning and afternoon

pikeperch.name: Pikeperch
pikeperch.overview: Pikeperch is a highly prized predator with delicate white flesh. It prefers deep, turbid waters and is mainly active at night, requiring specific fishing techniques.
pikeperch.habitat: Large deep lakes, reservoirs, lower stretches of rivers with silty or sandy beds.
pikeperch.behavior: Nocturnal predator; stays at depth during the day. Hunts in groups, driving prey towards the shore.
pikeperch.fishing: Spinning (soft plastics, deep-diving jerkbaits), dead-bait fishing, trolling. Key is presenting the lure close to the bottom.
pikeperch.season: Year-round, except the closed season (1 Jan – 31 May). Best in autumn.
pikeperch.diet: Fish (bleak, smelt, ruffe); prefers slender prey.
pikeperch.appearance: Elongated, fusiform body in shades of grey and green. Characteristic canine teeth.
pikeperch.spawning: April–May at 12–18°C on sandy or gravelly substrate.
pikeperch.bestBait: Soft plastic (shads, rippers), jerkbait, dead bait

carp.name: Carp
carp.overview: Carp is one of the most important species in Polish angling culture. Large, powerful and cunning, it is a serious challenge even for experienced carp anglers.
carp.habitat: Warm, still or slow-moving waters with muddy beds. Prefers ponds, lakes and oxbow lakes.
carp.behavior: Feeds near the bottom, actively digging through sediment. Cautious and easily spooked.
carp.fishing: Bottom and float fishing with specialist carp tackle. Baiting and bait selection (boilies, maize) are key.
carp.season: May–October (best in summer). Activity drops significantly in winter.
carp.diet: Benthic invertebrates, insect larvae, plant matter, molluscs.
carp.appearance: Massive, humped body covered with large scales (fully scaled) or partly naked (mirror carp). Golden-brown colouring.
carp.spawning: May–June at 18–20°C in shallow, vegetated areas.
carp.bestTime: Early morning and evening, warm nights

brownTrout.name: Brown Trout
brownTrout.overview: Brown trout is a beautiful, spirited fish inhabiting clean, cold mountain and foothill streams. It requires crystal-clear, well-oxygenated water.
brownTrout.habitat: Fast-flowing mountain and foothill streams with stony beds, cold lakes. Requires high dissolved oxygen levels.
brownTrout.behavior: Territorial; occupies lies behind boulders and in shaded spots. Actively rises to take insects on the surface.
brownTrout.fishing: Fly fishing (the primary method), ultralight spinning (small spinners, jerkbaits). Requires delicate tackle and precision.
brownTrout.season: March–August (depending on the fishing district). Closed season: 1 Sep – 31 Dec.
brownTrout.diet: Aquatic and terrestrial insects, crustaceans, small fish.
brownTrout.appearance: Slender body with characteristic red and black spots on the sides. Adipose dorsal fin.
brownTrout.spawning: October–January on gravel in cold, fast-flowing water.
brownTrout.bestTime: Morning and evening, after rain when water colours up

catfish.name: Wels Catfish
catfish.overview: The wels catfish is Europe's largest freshwater fish. It can reach impressive sizes and is a dream catch for many anglers. Targeting wels requires heavy tackle and great patience.
catfish.habitat: Large, deep rivers, reservoirs, lakes. Prefers spots with submerged obstacles and deep holes.
catfish.behavior: Mainly active at night. Spends the day in deep lairs. Ambush predator that uses its barbels to locate prey.
catfish.fishing: Bottom fishing with live bait, spinning (large soft plastics, jerkbaits), vertical jigging. Very heavy tackle required.
catfish.season: May–October (best June–August). Closed season: 1 Jan – 31 May.
catfish.appearance: Massive, scale-less body; broad, flat head with characteristic long barbels. Olive-brown colouring.
catfish.spawning: May–July at 20–24°C in shallow, vegetated bays.
catfish.bestBait: Live bait, dead bait, large soft plastic, bunch of worms
catfish.bestTime: Night, early dawn, stormy overcast days

roach.name: Roach
roach.overview: Roach is one of the most widespread fish in Poland, occurring in abundance across diverse waters. It is an excellent species for beginner anglers.
roach.habitat: Ubiquitous — lakes, rivers, canals, ponds, reservoirs. Prefers still and slow-flowing waters.
roach.behavior: Forms large shoals. Feeds mainly in mid-water and near the bottom. Active throughout the day.
roach.fishing: Float and bottom fishing. Takes small natural and plant baits readily.
roach.season: Year-round (no closed season). Best bites in spring and autumn.
roach.diet: Invertebrates, insect larvae, algae, plant matter.
roach.appearance: Slightly laterally compressed, silvery body. Reddish fins and orange iris.
roach.spawning: April–May at 10–15°C in shallow, vegetated areas.

eel.name: Eel
eel.overview: The eel is a mysterious, snake-like fish with a fascinating life cycle. It undertakes long migrations to spawn in the Sargasso Sea.
eel.habitat: Still and slow-flowing waters with muddy beds. Found in lakes, ponds and rivers.
eel.behavior: Nocturnal. Spends the day buried in mud. Active from spring to autumn; enters torpor in winter.
eel.fishing: Bottom fishing with live bait or dead bait, float fishing on the bottom. Best results at night.
eel.season: May–October. Closed season: 1 Dec – 31 Mar (minimum size 50 cm).
eel.diet: Benthic invertebrates, small fish, frogs, carrion.
eel.appearance: Long, snake-like body covered in slime. Dark green or brown on the back, yellowish on the belly.
eel.spawning: Spawns in the Sargasso Sea at approx. 400–700 m depth. Adults die after spawning.
eel.bestBait: Live bait, dead bait, earthworm, bunch of worms
